questions.question_text: Which set of ordered pairs represents a function?
questions.explanation: A function has each input (x-value) paired with exactly one output. Only {(1,2), (2,3), (3,4)} has no repeated x-values, so it is a function.

questions.question_text: A graph passes the vertical line test. What does this tell you?

options.text: The graph is a straight line
The graph represents a function
The graph has a positive slope
The graph is a relation but not a function

questions.explanation: If every vertical line drawn intersects the graph at most once, the graph passes the vertical line test and represents a function.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a function?

options.text: x = y^2
y = x^2
y = -3x

questions.explanation: x = y^2 gives two y-values for positive x-values (e.g., x = 4 gives y = 2 and y = -2), so it fails the vertical line test and is not a function.

questions.question_text: In the relation {(2, 5), (3, 7), (4, 5), (5, 9)}, what is the range?
questions.explanation: The range is the set of all output (y) values: {5, 7, 5, 9}. Removing duplicates gives {5, 7, 9}.

questions.question_text: Which table represents a function?
questions.explanation: A function requires each x-value to map to exactly one y-value. The table x: 1,2,3,4 → y: 4,4,4,4 has unique x-values, so it is a function (multiple x-values can share the same y-value).

questions.question_text: What is the domain of the relation {(-3, 1), (0, 4), (2, -1), (5, 3)}?
questions.explanation: The domain is the set of all input (x) values: {-3, 0, 2, 5}.

questions.question_text: A mapping diagram shows input 3 going to outputs 5 and 8. Is this a function?

options.text: Yes, because 3 is a valid input
No, because the input 3 is paired with two different outputs
Yes, because both outputs are positive
It depends on the other inputs

questions.explanation: In a function, each input can have only one output. Since 3 maps to both 5 and 8, this is not a function.

questions.question_text: A circle graphed on a coordinate plane fails the vertical line test. Why?

options.text: Because a circle has no x-values
Because most vertical lines through the circle intersect it at two points
Because a circle is not a relation
Because circles have negative slopes

questions.explanation: Most vertical lines drawn through a circle will cross it at two points, meaning there are x-values with two y-values, which violates the definition of a function.

questions.question_text: Is every function a relation?

options.text: No, functions and relations are unrelated concepts
Yes, every function is a relation, but not every relation is a function
No, relations are a subset of functions
Yes, and every relation is also a function

questions.explanation: A function is a special type of relation where each input has exactly one output. Every function is a relation, but a relation is only a function if it meets this additional requirement.

questions.question_text: The set {(1, 3), (2, 6), (3, 9), (4, 12)} represents a function. What is the output when the input is 3?
questions.explanation: The ordered pair (3, 9) shows that when the input is 3, the output is 9.

questions.question_text: What is a relation in mathematics?

options.text: A set of ordered pairs that pairs each input with exactly one output
Any set of ordered pairs that shows a connection between two sets of values
A linear equation in two variables
An equation that passes the vertical line test

questions.explanation: A relation is any set of ordered pairs. It describes a connection between inputs and outputs, without the restriction that each input maps to only one output.

questions.question_text: What is a function?

options.text: Any equation that can be graphed
A relation in which each input is paired with exactly one output
A set of ordered pairs where outputs can repeat
A graph that is always a straight line

questions.explanation: A function is a special relation where every input (x-value) has exactly one output (y-value). Multiple inputs may share the same output, but no input can have multiple outputs.

questions.question_text: What is the 'vertical line test'?

options.text: A method to determine the slope of a line
A visual test where vertical lines are drawn through a graph; if any vertical line hits the graph more than once, it is not a function
A test to find the y-intercept
A test to determine if two lines are parallel

questions.explanation: The vertical line test determines whether a graph represents a function. If any vertical line intersects the graph at more than one point, the graph does not represent a function.

questions.question_text: What does the term 'domain' mean?

options.text: The set of all output values of a relation or function
The set of all input values (x-values) of a relation or function
The slope of a linear function
The y-intercept of a function

questions.explanation: The domain is the set of all possible input values (x-values) for which the relation or function is defined.

questions.question_text: What does the term 'range' mean in the context of functions?

options.text: The set of all input values
The difference between the maximum and minimum values
The set of all output values (y-values) of a function
The equation of the function

questions.explanation: The range of a function is the set of all output values (y-values) that the function produces.

questions.question_text: What is a 'mapping diagram'?

options.text: A graph of a linear equation
A visual representation that uses arrows to show how each input is paired with an output
A type of scatter plot
A table of values with only one column

questions.explanation: A mapping diagram shows inputs on one side and outputs on the other, with arrows connecting each input to its corresponding output(s).

questions.question_text: What does 'input' mean in the context of a function?

options.text: The y-value or dependent variable
The x-value or independent variable that is substituted into the function
The equation of the function itself
The graph of the function

questions.explanation: The input is the x-value (independent variable) that you substitute into a function to produce an output (y-value).

questions.question_text: What does 'output' mean in the context of a function?

options.text: The independent variable
The result (y-value) produced by a function for a given input
The domain of the function
The equation used to define the function

questions.explanation: The output is the y-value (dependent variable) that results when a specific input is substituted into the function.

questions.question_text: What does 'ordered pair' mean?

options.text: A pair of numbers arranged in a specific order, typically (x, y), representing a point or an input-output pair
Any two numbers listed next to each other
A pair of equations solved simultaneously
Two points that share the same y-value

questions.explanation: An ordered pair is a pair of values written in a specific order (x, y), where the first value represents the input and the second represents the output.

questions.question_text: What does 'one-to-one function' mean?

options.text: A function where each input has one output and each output has exactly one input
A function where all outputs are the same
A function that is always linear
A function that only has one ordered pair

questions.explanation: A one-to-one function pairs each input with a unique output and each output with a unique input, meaning no two different inputs produce the same output.

questions.question_text: A vending machine takes a code (A1, A2, B1, B2) and dispenses a specific snack for each code. Is this vending machine an example of a function? Explain why.

options.text: No, because multiple codes could give the same snack
Yes, because each code (input) produces exactly one snack (output)
No, because vending machines are not mathematical
Yes, but only if each snack is different

questions.explanation: Each code maps to exactly one snack, satisfying the definition of a function. It does not matter if two different codes give the same snack; what matters is that each input has one output.

questions.question_text: A teacher assigns student ID numbers. Each student gets a unique ID, but siblings share the same last name. Is the pairing of student names to ID numbers a function? What about ID numbers to last names?

options.text: Both are functions
Names to IDs is a function (each name maps to one ID); IDs to last names is also a function (each ID maps to one last name)
Neither is a function
Only IDs to names is a function

questions.explanation: Each student name maps to exactly one ID (function). Each unique ID maps to exactly one student and thus one last name (also a function). Both pairings satisfy the function definition.

questions.question_text: A phone company charges $0.10 per text message. The total cost is a function of the number of texts sent. If a customer sends 150 texts, what is the total cost? Is the reverse (cost to number of texts) also a function?

options.text: $15.00; yes, the reverse is also a function because each cost corresponds to exactly one number of texts
$15.00; no, the reverse is not a function
$1.50; yes, the reverse is a function
$150.00; no, the reverse is not a function

questions.explanation: Cost = 0.10 x 150 = $15.00. Since each cost uniquely determines the number of texts (divide by 0.10), the reverse is also a function.

questions.question_text: A social media platform allows one username per account, but multiple accounts can have the same display name. Is the relation from usernames to display names a function?

options.text: No, because display names repeat
Yes, because each username (input) maps to exactly one display name (output)
No, because usernames must be unique
Only if every display name is different

questions.explanation: Each username maps to exactly one display name, which satisfies the function definition. It is fine for multiple inputs to share the same output.

questions.question_text: A weather station records the temperature at noon each day. Day 1: 72 degrees F, Day 2: 68 degrees F, Day 3: 72 degrees F, Day 4: 75 degrees F. Is this a function from day to temperature? Is it one-to-one?

options.text: Yes, it is a function and it is one-to-one
Yes, it is a function, but it is not one-to-one because Day 1 and Day 3 share the same temperature
No, it is not a function because 72 appears twice
No, it is not a function or one-to-one

questions.explanation: Each day maps to exactly one temperature, so it is a function. However, Days 1 and 3 both map to 72, so different inputs share the same output, making it not one-to-one.

questions.question_text: A bank assigns each customer a unique account number. A customer can have multiple accounts. Is the relation from customers to account numbers a function?

options.text: Yes, because each customer has accounts
No, because a single customer can map to multiple account numbers, violating the one-output rule
Yes, because account numbers are unique
No, because customers are not numbers

questions.explanation: Since one customer (input) can have multiple account numbers (outputs), this relation is not a function. A function requires each input to map to exactly one output.

questions.question_text: A car rental company charges a flat fee of $50 plus $0.20 per mile driven. Total cost is a function of miles driven (m). Write the function rule and find the cost for 200 miles.
A school maps each student to their homeroom teacher. Some teachers have 25 students. Is this a function from students to teachers?

options.text: No, because teachers have multiple students
Yes, because each student is assigned to exactly one homeroom teacher
No, because 25 is too many students per class
Only if each teacher has the same number of students

questions.explanation: Each student (input) is assigned to exactly one homeroom teacher (output). Multiple students sharing the same teacher does not violate the function definition.

questions.question_text: A delivery app assigns each order to one driver, but each driver can handle multiple orders. Is the relation from orders to drivers a function? What about from drivers to orders?

options.text: Orders to drivers: yes (each order has one driver). Drivers to orders: no (each driver may have multiple orders)
Both are functions
Neither is a function
Drivers to orders: yes. Orders to drivers: no

questions.explanation: Each order maps to one driver, making orders-to-drivers a function. But one driver can have multiple orders, so drivers-to-orders is not a function.

questions.question_text: A gym membership costs $30/month. The total cost C is a function of the number of months m: C(m) = 30m. After 8 months, you want to know how many months correspond to a $360 total. Is the inverse relation (cost to months) also a function?

options.text: C(8) = $240; yes, the inverse is a function because m = C/30 gives a unique month count for each cost
C(8) = $240; no, the inverse is not a function
C(8) = $360; yes, the inverse is a function
C(8) = $240; the inverse is undefined

questions.explanation: C(8) = 30(8) = $240. The inverse m = C/30 gives exactly one month count for each cost, so it is also a function. For $360: m = 360/30 = 12 months.
